title: ERC1271 RPC sign-in path with gas-burn defense against on-chain replay
trigger_patterns: burn gas to detect off-chain call
explanation: Allows wallet clients to verify a smart-account signature via plain eth_call without performing nested EIP-712 wrapping, while making on-chain replay computationally infeasible. (1) tx.gasprice == 0 is the heuristic for an off-chain RPC call. (2) The basefee precompile at 0x000000000000378eDCD5B5B0A24f5342d8C10485 is queried — if gasprice < basefee, definitely off-chain (eth_call lets gasprice be anything). (3) Otherwise, a recursive staticcall is made requesting gas > gaslimit; on-chain transactions are bounded by the block gaslimit, so this guarantees an out-of-gas revert if attempted on-chain, while RPC providers typically accept much larger gas budgets. (4) gasprice() is reused as a literal 0 throughout to save the `0x00` push opcode where an empty memory write is needed. (5) Variable b is reused as both basefee contract address and as a sanity hash to compare against (preventing self-referential recursion).
when_to_apply: Smart accounts that want to support easy off-chain sign-ins (Sign-In with Ethereum) without nested EIP-712 wrapping but must prevent on-chain replay
when_not_to_apply: Accounts where the same signature may legitimately be used both off-chain and on-chain, or chains without a basefee precompile
